_comment: Shared golden cases for the deferral-disposition classifier. Consumed by tools/tests/test_policy.py (the bin/policy check) AND tools/tests/test_block_defer_language.py (the .claude/hooks/block-defer-language.py hook), so the hook's self-contained classifier and the policy classifier cannot drift without a test failing. surface ∈ {issue-create, issue-edit, issue-close, issue-comment, pr-create, pr-edit, pr-comment, pr-body}. expect ∈ {deny, allow}. See ADR-029 and issue #830.

cases.text: Auth caching is deferred to a follow-up PR.
cases.why: Tier 1: explicit forward-deferral disposition. Forbidden on every surface.

cases.text: The token rotation is deferred to a follow-up issue.
cases.why: Tier 1 is forbidden even when creating a new issue.

cases.text: Will land the missing tests in a subsequent PR.
cases.why: Tier 1: 'in a subsequent PR'.

cases.text: Remaining lint findings will be addressed in a follow-up.
cases.why: Tier 1: 'will be addressed in a follow-up'.

cases.text: Fixed the obvious site; the rest fixed in a subsequent PR.
cases.why: Tier 1: 'fixed in a subsequent PR'.

cases.text: Defer this until a later iteration.
cases.why: Tier 1: 'defer ... until a later iteration'.

cases.text: The cleanup is handled as a follow-up issue.
cases.why: Tier 1: 'handled as a follow-up issue'.

cases.text: Two findings fixed; the third deferred.
cases.why: Tier 2: bare 'deferred' on a closing/commenting surface for the issue under implementation.

cases.text: Closing — remaining work deferred.
cases.why: Tier 2: bare 'deferred' on issue-close.

cases.text: Coverage for the edge case: TBD later.
cases.why: Tier 2: 'TBD later' postponement on a comment surface.

cases.text: The schema work is to be filed separately.
cases.why: Tier 2: 'to be filed separately'.

cases.text: This work was deferred from #828 and now needs doing.
cases.why: Bare 'deferred from #828' is a historical note, not a forward-deferral; and Tier 2 does not apply to issue-create at all.

cases.text: Builds on work deferred from the original cutover.
cases.why: Bare 'deferred' is Tier 2; Tier 2 does not apply to pr-body.

cases.text: We must not defer any of these findings.
cases.why: Negation guard: 'must not defer' is the rule being stated, not a deferral.

cases.text: Per the contract, never defer a reviewer finding.
cases.why: Negation guard: 'never defer'.

cases.text:
## Out of scope

- Retroactive cleanup of past issues.
cases.why: A PR body legitimately scope-bounds its own work with an '## Out of scope' section; no deferral verb.

cases.text:
## Out of scope

- Changing the existing hard cap behavior.
cases.why: A new issue legitimately bounds its own scope.

cases.text: These are out of scope and deferred to a follow-up issue.
cases.why: Tier 1: 'deferred to a follow-up issue' — caught regardless of the 'out of scope' framing.

cases.text: All review findings fixed; CI green; PR ready for merge.
cases.why: No deferral language.

cases.text: Implements the auth gate. All cross-cutting layers reused. Tests added.
cases.why: No deferral language.

cases.text: - [x] `gc_run_sweep` reviewed; findings fixed or recorded with rationale
cases.why: The amended Ground Control Checks template line — no 'deferred' wording after the A4 fix.

cases.text: Finding 3: wontfix — false positive on this codebase, see rationale below.
cases.why: A valid disposition recorded with rationale.

cases.text: Note: the SonarCloud findings are deferred to a follow-up PR.
cases.why: Tier 1 applies on pr-create too.

cases.text: PR #834 (a sibling cleanup) carries the doc updates.
cases.why: Referencing a sibling PR is not deferral; no deferral verb, no 'follow-up issue/PR' disposition phrase.
